## Pitfalls

- **Answer-first for terse follow-ups:** In algebra/physics derivations, if the user asks “what’s the answer?” or repeats the request, stop expanding context. Give the boxed formula/verdict first, then at most a short explanation.
- **SU(3) coordinate convention check:** After fixing `H=e7`, do not assume a generic pairing like `(e1+i e4, e2+i e5, e3+i e6)`. Verify the multiplication table. In the current Cayley-Dickson source of truth, the compatible coordinates are `(u1+i u6, u2-i u5, u3-i u4)`, and `[u,v,e7]_{C^3}=2i conjugate(z_u×z_v)`, so the norm is `4||z_u×z_v||²`, not `||Im(z_u×z_v)||²`.
- **Metadata ranking mistaken for audit:** When ranking notebooks/artifacts from a spreadsheet or file listing, metadata (title, size, date, category) can choose a first candidate, but it is not a deep dive. If the user asks which entry is best, open/read the top pick and verify its core claims before calling it trustworthy. If the candidate has the best design but fails verification, say **best repair target**, not “best evidence.”
- **Hard-coded algebra tables in notebooks:** Treat hand-written Cayley/Fano/octonion tables as suspect until basic laws pass. A notebook can have a polished “audit” title and still fail anti-commutativity, alternativity, or norm multiplicativity. See `references/octogan-top-pick-deep-dive.md` for a concrete OctoGAN failure pattern and audit checklist.
- **Scale-normalization loophole:** A “normalized” associator score must be checked by scaling the field (e.g. `field` vs `10*field`). Since `||[a,b,c]||²` scales as amplitude⁶, denominators based on `energy²` still scale as amplitude² and reward blow-up. Use amplitude⁶-compatible normalization or report the metric as amplitude-biased.
- **Mixing sources of truth:** If the user names one canonical file, do not substitute an older or alternate implementation even if it appears cleaner.
- **Dependency loops in sandbox:** Repeated imports of a torch-dependent module inside `execute_code` cannot resolve missing torch by re-importing. Execute the script in `terminal`.
- **Sandbox path substitution:** `/mnt/data`, `/tmp`, `/workspace`, and cloud-sandbox paths are not guaranteed to be mounted or current on the user's machine. When the user designates a local Windows repo as canonical, treat any non-local path as untrusted unless the user explicitly maps it. Never silently switch execution or source-of-truth to a sandbox path when the user has named a local canonical repo.
- **Artifact-gap hard stop:** Before running verification, enumerate every required file and confirm it exists in the canonical local repo. If any file is missing, stop and report the missing filenames; do not invent partial substitutions or rerun with an alternate source. Wait for the user to supply the missing artifacts.
- **Degenerate chart pitfalls:**
  - Random rank-1 center finding can iterate to failure; prefer constructing the center directly as `rank1(v1, v2, v3)` and verifying `sharp(X) == 0`.
  - Ambient tangent projections onto `X#=0` can yield a singular induced metric; if `det(g) < 1e-12`, switch to an explicit associative chart (e.g. `C = span{1, e6}`) or lower dimension.
  - 2D finite-difference charts are fragile at `diag(1,1,1)` because the induced metric can be degenerate; 1D centered-difference probes are the stable fallback.
- **Path case on Windows:** “ToE-21st-june-newest” != “ToE_21st_June_NEWEST”. Use the exact case the user supplies.
- **Identity-gate as theater:** Identity checks must appear in the result with numeric error values, not as qualitative prose-only assertions.
- **Topological action mistaken for dynamical gravity:** In 4D, `∫ R∧R` (Euler/Pontryagin density) is topological — it gives zero field equations. Only `∫ ε_{abcd} R^{ab} ∧ e^c ∧ e^d` (Einstein–Hilbert) yields dynamical gravity. Writing the former when claiming “gravity” is a fatal error; verify the action has the tetrad wedges before declaring Einstein equations.
- **Associator 3-form from product constants, not K-tensor:** The octonion-valued 3-form Θ must be built from the associator tensor `K_{abc}^d` (defined by `[e_a, e_b, e_c] = K_{abc}^d e_d`), not from the product structure constants `ψ_{abc}`. Fano-line triples have `K=0` (associative); non-Fano triples have `K≠0`. Conflating the two produces false-zero associators or spurious ones.
- **Associator tensor is NOT a multiplication table (caught 2026-07-13):** `mechanism_test.B_EXACT[i,j,k]` is the `k`-th imaginary component of `[e_{i+1}, e_{j+1}, e_7]` — the *associator* with `e_7` fixed, NOT the product `e_i * e_j`. Using `B @ e1 @ e2` as the octonion product silently produces a non-pure-imaginary, non-antisymmetric "associator" (real part survives, `[a,b,H]≠-[b,a,H]`). **Fix:** multiply through the locked Fano table `_fano_table()` with full 8-vector arithmetic (real part `−a·b` carried through), then take the associator as `((ab)c − a(bc))[1:]`. Reusable faithful kernel in `references/j3o-build-J-recipe.md`. Symptom to watch: an associator check that "should be pure-imaginary" returns a large component at index 0, or antisymmetry fails at ~0.6 — that is the B_EXACT-as-multiply error, not a convention bug.
- **7-vec vs 8-vec octonion arithmetic (caught 2026-07-13):** A pure-imaginary 7-vec has NO stored real part, so `e_i * e_i = −1` (a real scalar) writes out-of-bounds when you only keep 7 slots. Always promote to an 8-vector (index 0 = real) for products, then drop back to 7 for storage. This is the correct companion to the associator-tensor pitfall above.
- **Isotropy cannot be faked from a fixed frame:** A positive-weighted ensemble of non-Fano triples mapped to static 3-planes does **not** produce a cosmological-constant-like stress tensor `T∝g_{μν}`. The linear algebra of 3-form templates requires **signed** superpositions (negative coefficients) to hit the vacuum-energy direction. The “wild-but-plausible” G₂/Fano-balanced shell fails for fixed frames; explicit diagonal ratio checks (`T_{μμ}/g_{μμ}`) must be equal for all μ before calling something isotropic.
- **3-form stress-energy formula:** For a real 3-form Θ, the canonical stress tensor is `T_{μν} = 2 Θ_{μαβ} Θ_{ν}^{αβ} − (1/6) g_{μν} Θ^2`. Using a different factor (e.g. 1 or 4 instead of 2) silently breaks proportionality tests.
- **Channel compression creating blind spots:** When an algebra has fewer basis slots than input channels and the paper compresses by averaging, the compressed channels can form a systematic detection blind spot. Non-responders clustering in a specific spatial focus (e.g. right-hemisphere seizures in a left-heavy encoding) are strong evidence the compression is the dominant failure mode. Test this via multi-instance split before concluding the algebraic biomarker is inadequate.
- **Alternative law hiding degenerate encodings:** `[a, a, b] = 0` means if encoding accidentally feeds the same signal into two octonion components, the associator is forced to zero regardless of signal content. Any cross-channel associator computation must verify the three probe values are genuinely distinct before treating a zero associator as “no non-associativity present”.
- **FTS moment-map Y-slot transformation (subtle, caught 2026-07-12):** For tau-real states `Psi=(1,1,X,conj X)` the `u(3)` action is `dX=[iA,X]`, but the Y slot must transform as `dY = conj(dX)`, **NOT** `[iA,Y]`. A direct commutator gives `-conj(dX)` and breaks the symplectic cancellation that makes `mu_Psi(iA)` real. Symptom: `mu` comes out purely imaginary, so `mu.real == 0` and the reconstruction self-test (`|mu_direct - Tr(H@A_k)|`) **passes trivially at 0** — a false PASS. Always assert `mu_reality` (`max|Im mu| < 1e-10`) as a SEPARATE check. Skeleton + locked wedge anchors in `references/fts-moment-map-extraction-skeleton.md`.
- **Locked Jordan-data gap — PENDING, never fabricate:** FTS extraction needs the up/down/lepton Jordan elements `J_u, J_d, J_ν, J_e` to turn a wedge `(r,s)` into a 3×3 mixing matrix. When absent the extractor must return `PENDING_LOCKED_DATA` and the scanner must REFUSE to emit any CKM/PMNS fit. Do not synthesize `J`'s; a `--demo` optimizer run on synthetic `J`'s must be tagged `SYNTHETIC_PROOF_OF_CODE`, not a physics result. See `references/locked-jordan-gap-decision.md` for the full decision tree, the global-floor test, and the honest-file generator shape.
  - **Do NOT claim "irrecoverable / degenerate / CKM-unreachable" without the global-floor test.** `reconstruct_H(X)` IS many-to-one (`X` slot → Hermitian observable, so `H→X` is singular), and blind Nelder-Mead forward-fitting DOES collapse to the documented 2+1 attractor (observed `fro≈0.44` vs real `6e-6`, angles ≈38°, `J_cp ratio=0`). BUT that proves only that *random/optimized search* can't stumble onto CKM — not that no pair exists. The map `Ju + R_Q·Jd → H → eigenvectors` is an underdetermined linear system, so a **constructed** representative reaching `fro≈0` is TRIVIAL (hard-wire CKM eigenvectors into `H`, solve `Ju + R_Q·Jd = H`). The global-floor test (20k random pairs → best `fro≈0.109`, far above `6e-6`) shows the preimage is a *measure-zero set*, which is the real reason blind fitting fails — and why a constructed rep is the only downstream route. A constructed rep is **circular** (CKM baked in) and zero evidence for the theory; label it `constructed_representative_NOT_derivation`.
  - **SEPARATE BUG (caught 2026-07-12): `reconstruct_H` itself is rank-3 degenerate — distinct from the missing-data gap.** As written, the map `X(18 real) → H(18 real)` has **image dimension 3** (SVD: 3 nonzero singular values, rest 0), and every random `X` yields `H` with eigenvalues exactly `(-a, 0, +a)` (traceless, rank-2, forced zero eigenvalue). Root cause: `moment_map_coefficient`/`_action_iA_on_state` only populate the 3 off-diagonal `mu` components; identity + diagonal-traceless `mu` components come out exactly zero, so `H` can never have 3 distinct nonzero eigenvalues. **Consequence: CKM (needs 3 distinct eigenvalues in both `Hu`,`Hd`) is mathematically IMPOSSIBLE through the current `extract_mixing` path.** This means the repo's own "VERIFIED" label on `extract_mixing` and the cited `fro≈1e-5` CKM localization are NOT reproducible by the code as it stands — either the code regressed after the claim, or the claim was never run through this exact function. **Triage order: check this degeneracy BEFORE attempting any `J`-construction or trusting the `fro≈1e-5` result.** Reusable probe (image-dim 3, forced (-a,0,+a)) + fix pointer (restore scalar-slot contributions so `H` is full 3-eigenvalue Hermitian) in `references/fts-reconstruct-h-degeneracy.md`. The honest-file generator `build_locked_jordan_from_frame.py` exists but correctly REFUSES success (quark `fro≈0.25`, lepton blocked on `pmns_targets.verified`). Do not write `locked_jordan_data.json` until `reconstruct_H` is fixed.
  - **Correct recovery route (only if the user insists on a real file):** the genuine `J_u, J_d` were produced **upstream** by the full complex-Jordan minimizer (`full-complex-jordan-yhalf-minimization.md`), which built `X_u = J_u + r J_d` *directly* from a real shared-7-frame `Q` via the associator→Jordan lift — and that `Q` was **never checkpointed**. So: (1) re-run the upstream optimizer and **checkpoint `Q`** (the irreplaceable lost object); (2) build `J_u=J_{h_u}`, `J_d=J_{h_d}` via the locked lift; (3) lepton `J_ν,J_e` remain an independent **diagnostic fit**, NOT unification; (4) write `locked_jordan_data.json` with honest status tags. Gate C's C1–C5 unification rows stay `NOT_IMPLEMENTED_PHYSICS` — two independently-fit pairs do NOT constitute a shared-frame derivation; that circularity is unavoidable unless the lepton side is derived from the *same* `Q`.
  - **Placeholder targets are a hard stop for that gate.** Gate C pulls PMNS targets from `pmns_targets.default_normal_ordering()`; those are EXPLICIT placeholders ("verify before publication"). If the file exists but still holds placeholder values, REFUSE to write `J_ν, J_e` and demand the user supply real cited PMNS numbers (edit `default_normal_ordering()`) first. A target file that exists but holds placeholder VALUES is treated like a missing artifact for that gate.

Do NOT push `J_ν,J_e` through the modulus proxy or any ad-hoc embedding to "fit PMNS" — that manufactures a forbidden result. Leave faithful PMNS extraction as the next documented gate. See `references/j3o-build-J-recipe.md` (Gate C C4 section).

  - **Hollow "VERIFIED" self-test — false ALL_PASS (caught 2026-07-12):** A suite can report `ALL_PASS: True` while never exercising the path under claim. `fts_moment_map_extraction.run_self_tests()` only checked `mu==Tr(H@A)` consistency, `mu` reality, `Ω` antisymmetry, then gated CKM on `PENDING_LOCKED_DATA` — so the degenerate `reconstruct_H` (image-dim 3, forced `(-a,0,+a)` eigenvalues) was never caught, because no test calls `eigh(H)` or compares to real CKM. **Rule: a "verified" label is only as strong as its weakest untested path. # Gate Verification

Systematic evidence-backed verification of claims using explicit gate structures.

## Trigger

Use when:
- User asks to "verify", "check", "audit", or "test" a claim
- A mathematical/physical/algebraic claim needs explicit PASS/FAIL evidence
- A literature survey must distinguish formalism, EFT examples, named geometries, uplifted solutions, and Hessian-ready constructions
- Required artifacts must be confirmed before running verification
- User establishes a "gate" pattern with sub-criteria

Do NOT use for:
- Simple syntax checks or linting
- Vague "does this work" questions without explicit criteria

## Required Workflow

### 1. Confirm Workspace Paths First

**Before any verification:**
- Identify the user's canonical local repo path
- Verify it exists and is accessible
- Do NOT assume `/mnt/data`, `/tmp`, `/workspace`, or any sandbox path is mounted locally
- If user says "use local repo as source of truth", confirm paths against that repo only

Pitfall: Silently substituting assumed sandbox paths for local repos breaks verification and wastes compute.

### 2. Verify Required Artifacts Before Running

List all required files explicitly. Check existence one-by-one if needed.

If any file is missing:
- STOP
- List the missing files
- Ask user to copy/download them into the canonical repo
- Do NOT proceed with partial substitution unless user explicitly approves

### 3. Define Gate Structure

Every verification must have:

```markdown
## Gate [X] — [Name]

**Claim under test:** [exact statement]

**Construction:** [how the claim is instantiated from verified components]

| Sub-gate | PASS criterion |
|---|---|
| [A1] — [name] | [exact pass test] |
| [A2] — [name] | [exact pass test] |
| [A3] — [name] | [exact pass test] |

**Honesty boundary:** [what passing does NOT prove]
```

### 4. Run Verification and Record Evidence

- Use the canonical local repo as working directory
- Run the verification script/module
- Capture stdout and exit code
- Read generated artifacts (JSON, MD reports)
- Do NOT claim verification without reading the actual output

### 5. Report Explicit PASS/FAIL

Report format:
- Gate number and name
- Sub-gate results (PASS/FAIL for each)
- Overall verdict: PASS or FAIL
- Evidence: key numbers from artifacts
- Honesty boundary: what the result does NOT prove

Keep user-facing verification summaries concise. Lead with the result and the smallest useful evidence set; avoid long explanatory digressions unless the user asks for the teaching version.

## Ad-Hoc Verification Scripts

When creating temporary verification scripts:

- Use OS temp directory: `C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Temp` on Windows
- Filename prefix: `hermes-verify-`
- Script purpose: targeted check of the changed behavior
- If a post-edit system check says no canonical command was detected, run the focused temp script even if you already ran tests; label the result explicitly as **ad-hoc verification**, not suite/CI green.
- After running: clean up the temp script
- If system feedback flags specific changed paths, verify those exact paths even if they are accidental copies; do not silently switch the verification target to the intended canonical path. If the flagged copy depends on canonical repo modules, import those dependencies explicitly and state that boundary in the verification summary.
- See `references/changed-path-ad-hoc-verification.md` for the changed-path verification pattern and tiny7m example.

Template pattern:
```python
import sys, json
from pathlib import Path

repo = Path(r"D:\Projects\...")
artifact = repo / "results.json"

ok = True
def check(cond, label):
    global ok
    if not cond:
        ok = False
        print(f"FAIL: {label}")
    else:
        print(f"OK: {label}")

# assertions...

print("VERIFICATION_RESULT:", "PASS" if ok else "FAIL")
sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)
```

## Pitfalls

1. **Sandbox path assumption**: Never assume `/mnt/data`, `/tmp`, `/workspace`, or cloud sandbox paths are locally mounted. Confirm with user first.

2. **Silent file substitution**: If a required artifact is missing, report it and stop. Do not substitute a "similar" file without explicit user approval.

3. **Synthetic dry-run ≠ real-data run**: When the user asks to run/download/evaluate **real** data, do not answer by generating synthetic-shaped data or a dry-run unless the user explicitly approves that fallback. First check for the real file/source and credentials; if absent, state the blocker plainly and ask for the missing credential/path. Synthetic harness validation may be useful, but label it as a separate preparatory check, not the requested real-data result.

4. **Claim without evidence**: Never say "verified" or "PASS" without reading the actual output artifacts. A script that runs is not a script that passes.

4a. **Harness says PASS while the claim fails**: Audit the pass-condition logic itself. A status such as `RUN`, `COMPLETED`, or `DATA_LOADED` proves only that execution occurred; it must not satisfy a scientific gate unless the claimed numerical thresholds are asserted. Recompute and print the headline metrics independently, inspect artifact metadata for circularity/proxy/reconstruction warnings, and fail the gate when those warnings invalidate provenance. Also verify that metric names match formulas (`sqrt(mean(error^2))` is RMSE, not a Frobenius norm).

5. **Over-broad verification**: Run targeted checks, not full suites, unless user requests it.

6. **Missing cleanup**: Remove temp verification scripts after use.

7. **Reproducibility gate on cited numeric anchors**: A claim that leans on a previously-reported number (e.g. "fro~1e-5 CKM", "99% accuracy") must be gated by re-deriving that number from the **actual artifact** before you build on it. A docstring/comment/summary line is not the anchor — scan the real trained checkpoints / run the real path. If the number is unreproducible (here: best frame-overlap fro = 0.32 across all 32 `.pt` files, vs claimed 1e-5), the gate FAILS and you must NOT align, refactor, or "complete the path" to a phantom anchor. 8. **User "GO FOR IT" / explicit direction does not waive the evidence gate**: When the user says "align Gate C to stage_h" or "go for it", treat it as authorization to *proceed with the verified plan*, not as permission to skip verification of the premise. {"content": "1|# Locked historical FTS diagnostic fit
2|
3|## Verdict
4|
5|The July 11 Hermes result is locked as a **reproduced historical diagnostic fit**, not a CKM derivation.
6|
7|Machine-readable source of truth: `locked_historical_fts_diagnostic_fit.json`.
8|
9|Recovered executable: `historical_hermes_general_fts_state_recovered.py`.
10|
11|Structural verifier: `python verify_locked_historical_fts_fit.py`.
12|
13|## What is locked
14|
15|- the recovered `Q_star` and its column interpretation;
16|- the historical associator-to-Jordan construction of `J_u,J_d`;
17|- the full compact octonion coefficients of `J_u,J_d`;
18|- the stationary frame-action statistics;
19|- fitted complex coefficients `r,s`;
20|- the recorded CKM magnitudes, Jarlskog invariant, angles, and error;
21|- database session/message provenance and source hashes.
22|
23|## Claim firewall
24|
25|Safe statement:
26|
27|> A recovered and rerun Hermes pipeline constructs `J_u,J_d` from a seeded, action-minimized shared frame. A subsequent four-real-parameter, CKM-targeted complex Jordan-slot fit reproduces the CKM reference with Frobenius error `6.1281124199e-6` and the target Jarlskog invariant.
28|
29|Unsafe statement:
30|
31|> The octonionic/FTS construction derives or predicts CKM.
32|
33|The unsafe statement is not supported because CKM information enters both the `Q0` warm start and the `r,s` objective.
34|
35|## Derivation gate
36|
37|The diagnostic fit is now frozen and must not be changed during a derivation attempt. A candidate selector passes only if:
38|
39|1. its objective contains no CKM magnitudes, mixing angles, Jarlskog target, fitted `r,s`, or distance to `Q_star`;
40|2. all coefficients and boundary charges are specified before looking at CKM scoring;
41|3. it selects `Q,r,s` from multiple independent starts;
42|4. CKM is evaluated only after selection;
43|5. the result is stable to convention checks and small numerical perturbations;
44|6. parameter counting and null baselines show that success is not generic fitting capacity.
45|
46|## Already failed selector families
47|
48|The Hermes history records negative gates for:
49|
50|- direct normalized FTS/Jordan invariant actions in `r,s`;
51|- simple cross-norm, determinant, symplectic, and moment-map pseudoscalar combinations;
52|- self-attractor, coupled-plane, and primitive-charge closures tested in the initial full-FTS attractor attempt.
53|
54|These failures do not make derivation logically impossible. They rule out the obvious low-complexity completions. A credible next attempt needs independently justified two-Higgs/GUT boundary charges or a larger `E7` orbit/attractor construction—not another objective reverse-engineered from the fitted point.

{"content": "1|"""Verify the immutable, non-predictive parts of the recovered Hermes fit lock.
2|
3|This script deliberately does not optimize against CKM. It verifies that the
4|locked orthogonal frame reconstructs the recorded octonionic J_u and J_d.
5|"""
6|
7|from __future__ import annotations
8|
9|import json
10|from pathlib import Path
11|
12|import numpy as np
13|
14|
15|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
16|LOCK = ROOT / "locked_historical_fts_diagnostic_fit.json"
17|
18|
19|def qmul(x: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
20|    a, b, c, d = x
21|    e, f, g, h = y
22|    return np.array(
23|        [
24|            a * e - b * f - c * g - d * h,
25|            a * f + b * e + c * h - d * g,
26|            a * g - b * h + c * e + d * f,
27|            a * h + b * g - c * f + d * e,
28|        ]
29|    )
30|
31|
32|def qconj(x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
33|    out = x.copy()
34|    out[1:] *= -1
35|    return out
36|
37|
38|def omul(x: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
39|    a, b = x[:4], x[4:]
40|    c, d = y[:4], y[4:]
41|    return np.r_[qmul(a, c) - qmul(qconj(d), b), qmul(d, a) + qmul(b, qconj(c))]
42|
43|
44|def assoc(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray, h: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
45|    def pure(v: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
46|        return np.r_[0.0, v]
47|
48|    return (omul(omul(pure(a), pure(b)), pure(h)) - omul(pure(a), omul(pure(b), pure(h))))[1:]
49|
50|
51|def build_compact_j(gen: np.ndarray, h: np.ndarray) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
52|    a01 = assoc(gen[:, 0], gen[:, 1], h)
53|    a12 = assoc(gen[:, 1], gen[:, 2], h)
54|    a20 = assoc(gen[:, 2], gen[:, 0], h)
55|    diagonal = np.array(
56|        [
57|            np.dot(a20, a20) + np.dot(a01, a01),
58|            np.dot(a01, a01) + np.dot(a12, a12),
59|            np.dot(a20, a20) + np.dot(a12, a12),
60|        ]
61|    )
62|    return {"diagonal": diagonal, "a01": a01, "a12": a12, "a20": a20}
63|
64|
65|def main() -> int:
66|    data = json.loads(LOCK.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
67|    q = np.asarray(data["Q_star"], dtype=float)
68|    gen = q[:, :3]
69|
70|    checks: dict[str, float | bool | str] = {
71|        "status_is_diagnostic_not_derivation": data["status"].endswith("NOT_DERIVATION"),
72|        "q_orthogonality_max_abs": float(np.max(np.abs(q.T @ q - np.eye(7)))),
73|        "q_determinant": float(np.linalg.det(q)),
74|    }
75|
76|    for label, col in (("J_u", 3), ("J_d", 5)):
77|        got = build_compact_j(gen, q[:, col])
78|        expected = data[label]
79|        checks[f"{label}_diagonal_max_abs"] = float(
80|            np.max(np.abs(got["diagonal"] - np.asarray(expected["diagonal"])))
81|        )
82|        for name in ("a01", "a12", "a20"):
83|            checks[f"{label}_{name}_max_abs"] = float(
84|                np.max(np.abs(got[name] - np.asarray(expected[f"{name}_e1_to_e7"])))
85|            )
86|
87|    tolerances = [v for k, v in checks.items() if k.endswith("max_abs")]
88|    passed = (
89|        bool(checks["status_is_diagnostic_not_derivation"])
90|        and abs(checks["q_determinant"] - 1.0) < 1e-10
91|        and all(float(v) < 1e-10 for v in tolerances)
92|    )
93|    checks["all_pass"] = passed
94|    print(json.dumps(checks, indent=2, sort_keys=True))
95|    return 0 if passed else 1
96|
97|
98|if __name__ == "__main__":
99|    raise SystemExit(main())
100|", "total_lines": 99, "file_size": 3053, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "1|import torch, numpy as np, itertools, math, sys
2|try:
3|    from scipy.optimize import minimize
4|    HAVE_SCIPY=True
5|except Exception:
6|    HAVE_SCIPY=False
7|
8|torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float64)
9|CKM_abs_np=np.array([[0.97435,0.22500,0.00369],[0.22486,0.97349,0.04182],[0.00857,0.04110,0.999118]],float)
10|CKM_abs=torch.tensor(CKM_abs_np)
11|perms=list(itertools.permutations(range(3)))
12|s12_t=0.22500; s23_t=0.04182; s13_t=0.00369
13|c12_t=math.sqrt(1-s12_t*s12_t); c23_t=math.sqrt(1-s23_t*s23_t); c13_t=math.sqrt(1-s13_t*s13_t)
14|a=s12_t*s23_t; b=c12_t*c23_t*s13_t; vtd=0.00857
15|cosd=(a*a+b*b-vtd*vtd)/(2*a*b); cosd=max(-1,min(1,cosd)); delta_t=math.acos(cosd)
16|J_target=s12_t*s23_t*s13_t*c12_t*c23_t*c13_t*c13_t*math.sin(delta_t)
17|# octonion tables
18|def qmul_np(x,y):
19|    a,b,c,d=x; e,f,g,h=y
20|    return np.array([a*e-b*f-c*g-d*h,a*f+b*e+c*h-d*g,a*g-b*h+c*e+d*f,a*h+b*g-c*f+d*e],dtype=np.result_type(x,y))
21|def qconj_np(x): z=x.copy(); z[1:]*=-1; return z
22|def omul_np(x,y):
23|    a,b=x[:4],x[4:]; c,d=y[:4],y[4:]
24|    return np.r_[qmul_np(a,c)-qmul_np(qconj_np(d),b),qmul_np(d,a)+qmul_np(b,qconj_np(c))]
25|E=np.eye(8); C_np=np.zeros((8,8,8)); A_np=np.zeros((7,7,7,7))
26|for i,j in itertools.product(range(8),repeat=2): C_np[i,j,:]=omul_np(E[i],E[j])
27|for i,j,h in itertools.product(range(7),repeat=3):
28|    A_np[i,j,h,:]=(omul_np(omul_np(E[i+1],E[j+1]),E[h+1])-omul_np(E[i+1],omul_np(E[j+1],E[h+1])))[1:]
29|C=torch.tensor(C_np); A=torch.tensor(A_np)
30|I3t=torch.zeros(3,3,8); I3t[0,0,0]=I3t[1,1,0]=I3t[2,2,0]=1.0
31|# torch optimization/action helpers
32|def q_frame(raw):
33|    Q,R=torch.linalg.qr(raw); s=torch.sign(torch.diagonal(R)); s=torch.where(s==0,torch.ones_like(s),s); return Q*s.unsqueeze(0)
34|def edge_t(gen,H): return torch.einsum('ia,jb,h,ijhk->abk',gen,gen,H,A)
35|def mass_from_T(T): return (T*T).sum(-1)
36|def B_from_T(Td,Tk): return 2*(Td*Tk).sum(-1)
37|def mass_t(gen,H): return mass_from_T(edge_t(gen,H))
38|def sorted_basis_real(M):
39|    w,V=torch.linalg.eigh(M); idx=torch.argsort(torch.abs(w),descending=True); return w[idx],V[:,idx]
40|row_perm=torch.tensor([1,2,0]); col_perm=torch.tensor([2,1,0])
41|def ckm_loss_real_fixed(Q):
42|    gen=Q[:,:3]; Mu=mass_t(gen,Q[:,3]); Md=mass_t(gen,Q[:,5]); _,U=sorted_basis_real(Mu); _,D=sorted_basis_real(Md)
43|    W=torch.abs((U.T@D)[row_perm][:,col_perm]); return ((W-CKM_abs)**2).sum(),W
44|def oct_conj_t(x): y=x.clone(); y[...,1:]=-y[...,1:]; return y
45|def mat_mul_t(X,Y): return torch.einsum('ikA,kjB,ABC->ijC',X,Y,C)
46|def jordan_t(X,Y): return 0.5*(mat_mul_t(X,Y)+mat_mul_t(Y,X))
47|def trJ_t(X): return X[0,0,0]+X[1,1,0]+X[2,2,0]
48|def innerJ_t(X,Y): return trJ_t(jordan_t(X,Y))
49|def sharp_t(X):
50|    X2=jordan_t(X,X); tr=trJ_t(X); S=0.5*(tr*tr-trJ_t(X2)); return X2-tr*X+S*I3t
51|def normJ_t(X): return torch.sqrt(torch.clamp(innerJ_t(X,X),min=0)+1e-12)
52|def detJ_t(X): return innerJ_t(X,sharp_t(X))/3.0
53|def assoc_vec_t(gen,H,a,b):
54|    v=torch.einsum('i,j,h,ijhk->k',gen[:,a],gen[:,b],H,A); return torch.cat([torch.zeros(1,dtype=v.dtype),v])
55|def build_J_t(gen,H):
56|    z=assoc_vec_t(gen,H,0,1); x=assoc_vec_t(gen,H,1,2); y=assoc_vec_t(gen,H,2,0)
57|    nx=(x*x).sum(); ny=(y*y).sum(); nz=(z*z).sum(); X=torch.zeros(3,3,8,dtype=gen.dtype)
58|    X[0,0,0]=ny+nz; X[1,1,0]=nz+nx; X[2,2,0]=ny+nx
59|    X[0,1]=oct_conj_t(z); X[1,0]=z; X[0,2]=oct_conj_t(y); X[2,0]=y; X[1,2]=oct_conj_t(x); X[2,1]=x
60|    return X
61|def Atwist(Q):
62|    gen=Q[:,:3]; Ju=build_J_t(gen,Q[:,3]); Jd=build_J_t(gen,Q[:,5]); Jun=Ju/(normJ_t(Ju)+1e-12); Jdn=Jd/(normJ_t(Jd)+1e-12)

{"success": true, "mode": "scroll", "session_id": "20260711_022434_8a1e75", "around_message_id": 4051, "session_meta": {"when": "July 11, 2026 at 02:24 AM", "source": "tui", "model": "stepfun/step-3.7-flash:free", "title": "SU(3) Reduction of Octonions"}, "window": 5, "messages": [{"id": 4046, "role": "tool", "content": "{"todos": [{"id": "write-fts-moment", "content": "Implement FTS symplectic form and u(3)_gen moment map", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "run-fts-moment", "content": "Run action-minimized frame through FTS moment-map projection variants", "status": "completed"}, {"id": "record-fts-moment", "content": "Clean script, save FTS moment-map gate result, report verdict", "status": "completed"}], "summary": {"total": 3, "pending": 0, "in_progress": 0, "completed": 3, "cancelled": 0}}", "timestamp": 1783738500.373828, "tool_name": "todo", "tool_call_id": "call_j60eDaUmiYKjkx3lt3Sol9UU"}, {"id": 4047, "role": "assistant", "content": "Implemented and ran it.

\[
\boxed{
\Omega,\ \tau,\ T\in u(3)_{\rm gen},\ H_u,\ H_d,\ \text{and diagonalization are now coded and tested.}
}
\]

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{
\text{FTS compact moment-map projection works as a projection, but the simplest sector states do not complete CKM.}
}
\]

---

# 1. What I implemented

Freudenthal state:

\[
\Psi_s=(1,1,X_s,\overline{X_s}).
\]

Real structure:

\[
\tau(\alpha,\beta,X,Y)
=
(\overline\beta,\overline\alpha,\overline Y,\overline X).
\]

Symplectic form:

\[
\Omega((a,b,X,Y),(c,d,Z,W))
=
ad-bc+\langle X,W\rangle-\langle Y,Z\rangle.
\]

For a sector state,

\[
\Psi=(1,1,X,\overline X),
\]

this becomes:

\[
\Omega(\tau\Psi,T\Psi)
=
\langle X,\overline{dX}\rangle
-
\langle\overline X,dX\rangle.
\]

Moment map:

\[
\boxed{
\mu_\Psi(T)
=
\frac{i}{2}
\Omega(\tau\Psi,T\Psi).
}
\]

I represented

\[
T\in u(3)_{\rm gen}
\]

as

\[
T=iA,
\qquad
A=A^\dagger.
\]

Then reconstructed the Hermitian generation observable \(H_\Psi\) from

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Tr}_3(H_\Psi A)=\mu_\Psi(iA).
}
\]

The \(u(3)_{\rm gen}\) action was applied to the generation frame columns \(g_a\), not as a post-hoc matrix patch:

\[
g\mapsto g\exp(\epsilon T).
\]

---

# 2. Verification diagnostics

The moment-map reconstruction passed.

Random Hermitian probe errors:

\[
\sim 4\times10^{-14}\text{ to }2\times10^{-13}.
\]

So the extracted \(H_\Psi\) really matches

\[
\mu_\Psi(T).
\]

Action-minimized frame reproduced:

\[
S_*=-0.473691374068,
\]

\[
\Delta-2N_d=-0.352199185598,
\]

\[
P_{\rm chiral}=0.242984376941,
\]

\[
\|\nabla S_*\|\approx1.78\times10^{-7}.
\]

---

# 3. Baseline FTS moment map: real sector states

Using

\[
X_u=J_u,
\qquad
X_d=J_d,
\]

the extracted observables were:

\[
H_u\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
163.359860&3.025054&-40.760144\\
3.025054&107.472634&-2.941030\\
-40.760144&-2.941030&160.189373
\end{pmatrix},
\]

\[
H_d\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
184.5992&1.123268&0.025377\\
1.123268&244.5975&1.051411\\
0.025377&1.051411&184.5950
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Eigenvalues:

\[
u:\quad
(202.75239499,\ 120.98369453,\ 107.28577729),
\]

\[
d:\quad
(244.63692394,\ 184.58314081,\ 184.57162856).
\]

Diagonalizing gives best CKM-magnitude permutation:

\[
|V|\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
0.99562284&0.09034180&0.02394843\\
0.08918675&0.99497214&0.04556501\\
0.02794445&0.04322968&0.99867427
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Frobenius distance:

\[
\boxed{0.195598}
\]

Jarlskog:

\[
\boxed{J=0.}
\]

So the real FTS moment map is clean but not CKM.

---

# 4. Compact real generation rotations

I also tested compact real generation rotations, e.g.

\[
g_d\mapsto g_d\exp(0.5L),
\qquad
L=[\widehat B_1,\widehat B_2].
\]

Best nearby real example:

\[
|V|\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
0.97104134&0.23567386&0.03919868\\
0.23324389&0.97068413&0.05804841\\
0.05173003&0.04722455&0.99754391
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Frobenius:

\[
\boxed{0.06025}
\]

but

\[
\boxed{J=0.}
\]

So compact real rotations can move the magnitudes closer, but they cannot generate CP.

---

# 5. Complexified generation variants

I tested complexified generation states:

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(icK),
\]

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(icL),
\]

and split variants:

\[
g_u\mapsto g\exp(-icG/2),
\qquad
g_d\mapsto g\exp(+icG/2).
\]

They do generate complex Hermitian \(H_u,H_d\), hence nonzero CP.

But they do not land on CKM.

Representative example:

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(i0.5K):
\]

\[
|V|\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
0.99676994&0.07013327&0.03912808\\
0.06836553&0.99662418&0.04545770\\
0.04214068&0.04267989&0.99819967
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

\[
\|\,|V|-|V_{\rm CKM}|\,\|_F
\approx0.22784,
\]

\[
\frac{|J|}{J_{\rm target}}
\approx0.69086.
\]

Best tested complex magnitude case was split \(G=L,\ c=1\):

\[
|V|\approx
\begin{pmatrix}
0.97662793&0.21125835&0.03959548\\
0.21162947&0.97633411&0.04454969\\
0.03756140&0.04627762&0.99822218
\end{pmatrix}.
\]

Frobenius:

\[
\boxed{0.05042}
\]

but CP is far too large:

\[
\boxed{
|J|/J_{\rm target}
\approx11.49.
}
\]

---

# 6. Gate verdict

Projection implementation:

\[
\boxed{\text{PASS}}
\]

Physics completion:

\[
\boxed{\text{FAIL for the simplest tested } \Psi_s=(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s}) \text{ states.}}
\]

The important thing is that this is now no longer vague. The actual projection is coded and verified:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Tr}_3(H_\Psi A)
=
\frac{i}{2}
\Omega(\tau\Psi,iA\Psi).
}
\]

And it gives real, diagonalizable Hermitian \(H_u,H_d\).

But the sector state itself is still incomplete.

---

# 7. What this means physically

The observable projection is now correct, but the **state inserted into the projection** is not yet the full physical FTS state.

The simple choice

\[
\Psi_s=(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s})
\]

is too poor.

It either gives:

- real states: decent structure, no CP;

(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s}).

"type": "function", "function": {"name": "skill_manage", "arguments": "{\"action\":\"write_file\",\"file_content\":\"# General FTS state gate: scalar slots vs complex Jordan slots\\n\\nUse after `fts-compact-moment-map-projection-gate.md` when testing whether the remaining missing piece is the full Freudenthal state\\n\\n```text\\nPsi = (alpha,beta,X,Y)\\n```\\n\\nrather than the projection map.\\n\\n## Claim under test\\n\\nThe user proposed replacing the minimal sector state\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_s = (1,1,J_s,conj(J_s))\\n```\\n\\nby a more physical FTS state with nontrivial scalar slots and independent Jordan slots, e.g.\\n\\n```text\\nPsi = (v_u,v_d,J_u,J_d).\\n```\\n\\n## Construction\\n\\nKept the already verified FTS compact moment map:\\n\\n```text\\nmu_Psi(T) = (i/2) Omega(tau(Psi),T Psi),  T in u(3)_gen\\n```\\n\\nwith\\n\\n```text\\nOmega((a,b,X,Y),(c,d,Z,W)) = a d - b c + <X,W> - <Y,Z>\\ntau(alpha,beta,X,Y) = (conj(beta),conj(alpha),conj(Y),conj(X))\\n```\\n\\nFor compact generation probes `T=iA`, `A=A†`, the implementation used the representation commuting with `tau`:\\n\\n```text\\nT X = dX,\\nT Y = conj-representation derivative dY.\\n```\\n\\nThe Hermitian generation observable was reconstructed from\\n\\n```text\\nTr_3(H_Psi A) = mu_Psi(iA).\\n```\\n\\n## Action-minimized frame\\n\\nSame stationary point:\\n\\n```text\\nS      = -0.47369137406837525\\nAtwist = -0.3521991855981212\\nDelta  = -0.10808374021302673\\nN_d    =  0.12205772269254724\\nP      =  0.24298437694050817\\ngrad   =  1.782443763352243e-07\\n```\\n\\n## Key algebraic finding: scalar slots are inert for u(3)_gen\\n\\nFor `T in u(3)_gen` acting only on generation/Jordan slots,\\n\\n```text\\nT alpha = 0,\\nT beta  = 0.\\n```\\n\\nTherefore the scalar part of the symplectic form contributes\\n\\n```text\\nalpha * delta(beta) - beta * delta(alpha) = 0.\\n```\\n\\nNumerical check:\\n\\n```text\\nALPHA_BETA_INVARIANCE max_delta_H_over_random_scalars 0.0\\n```\\n\\nSo changing `alpha,beta` alone cannot change CKM under this moment-map projection. They may affect a larger E7 state-selection action or central/trace sector, but they do not alter the generation eigenvectors for `u(3)_gen`.\\n\\n## Independent raw slots Psi=(alpha,beta,J_u,J_d)\\n\\nA single combined state\\n\\n```text\\nPsi=(alpha,beta,J_u,J_d)\\n```\\n\\nproduces only one moment-map observable `H`, not two sector observables. The computed combined observable was real:\\n\\n```text\\nH_combined ≈\\n[[173.979543,   2.074161, -20.367384],\\n [  2.074161, 176.035057,  -0.944810],\\n [-20.367384,  -0.944810, 172.392181]]\\n```\\n\\nSlot-separated observables from `X=J_u` and `Y=J_d` reproduce the earlier real-sector no-CP result:\\n\\n```text\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.99562284,0.09034180,0.02394843],\\n [0.08918675,0.99497214,0.04556501],\\n [0.02794445,0.04322968,0.99867427]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 0.195598\\nJ/J_target = 0\\n```\\n\\nSwapping combined states `Psi_u=(J_u,J_d)`, `Psi_d=(J_d,J_u)` gives identity mixing and `J=0`.\\n\\n## Non-real independent slot search without slot interference\\n\\nDiagnostic ansatz:\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_u=(J_u, r J_d),\\nPsi_d=(s J_u, J_d)\\n```\\n\\ncan fit real magnitudes well but still gives no CP because each slot contribution scales without complex interference:\\n\\n```text\\nbest r ≈ -0.247973 - 0.378564 i\\nbest s ≈ -0.988994 + 0.492332 i\\n\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.97437895,0.22472872,0.00909220],\\n [0.22491247,0.97354413,0.04032631],\\n [0.00021082,0.04133806,0.99914520]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 0.010071\\nJ/J_target ≈ 5.8e-18\\n```\\n\\n## Decisive positive diagnostic: complex Jordan-slot mixing\\n\\nAllow the sector states to be tau-real but with complex linear mixing inside the Jordan slot:\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_u:\\n  X_u = J_u + r J_d\\n  Y_u = conj(X_u)\\n\\nPsi_d:\\n  X_d = s J_u + J_d\\n  Y_d = conj(X_d)\\n```\\n\\nA 4-real-parameter diagnostic search found:\\n\\n```text\\nr ≈ 0.3887831255168539 + 0.28228702817500606 i\\ns ≈ 0.23729498924317205 - 0.5117538711322562 i\\n```\\n\\nand produced essentially exact CKM magnitudes and J:\\n\\n```text\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.97435246,0.22499704,0.00369001],\\n [0.22486405,0.97349226,0.04182082],\\n [0.00856999,0.04109930,0.99911831]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 6.13e-06\\nJ ≈ 3.076559155801642e-05\\nJ/J_target ≈ 1.0000000017619248\\n\\ns12 ≈ 0.2249985724\\ns23 ≈ 0.0418211054\\ns13 ≈ 0.0036900121\\n```\\n\\n## Verdict\\n\\nProjection gate:\\n\\n```text\\nPASS: FTS moment map still works.\\nPASS: alpha,beta invariance under u(3)_gen verified.\\nPASS: independent Jordan slots implemented.\\nPASS: complex tau-real Jordan-slot mixing can reproduce CKM as a diagnostic fit.\\n```\\n\\nPhysics/derivation gate:\\n\\n```text\\nFAIL: scalar slots alone do not solve CKM.\\nFAIL: raw Psi=(v_u,v_d,J_u,J_d) does not itself provide two CKM sector observables or CP.\\nNOT YET PROVEN: the complex mixing coefficients r,s are derived by the action.\\n```\\n\\n## Honesty boundary\\n\\nThe near-exact CKM result is a fitted 4-real-parameter diagnostic, not a zero-parameter derivation. Its value is that it localizes the missing physics precisely: the physical FTS state must contain complex tau-real Jordan-slot mixing of `J_u` and `J_d`. The next gate is to derive `r,s` from an invariant FTS state-selection equation, not to tune them to CKM.\\n\\nPossible next action: extend the action from `S(Q)` on the shared frame to `S(Q,r,s)` on the FTS state and test whether its stationary equations recover the fitted `r,s` without CKM input.\\n\",\"file_path\":\"references/general-fts-state-jordan-slot-mixing-gate.md\",\"name\":\"verified-algebra-workflow\"}"}}]}], "messages_before": 5, "messages_after": 5}

3].astype(np.complex128)\\nvacd = q[:, 5].astype(np.complex128)\\ncache = ns[\\\"SlotCache\\\"](gen, [vacu, vacd])\\n\\nrj = data[\\\"diagnostic_fit\\\"][\\\"r\\\"]\\nsj = state_H_fast(cache, [1, r], [1, np.conjugate(r)])\\nHd = state_H_fast(cache, [s, 1], [np.conjugate(s), 1])\\nrec = ns[\\\"best_from_H\\\"](Hu, Hd)\\nfro, jarl, s12, s23, s13, row_perm, col_perm, vabs = rec[:8]\\nlocked = data[\\\"diagnostic_fit\\\"]\\n\\n# Recompute the frame action and its local 21-angle gradient at the locked Q.\\nqt = torch.tensor(q, dtype=torch.float64)\\ntheta = torch.zeros(21, dtype=torch.float64, requires_grad=True)\\nqtheta = ns[\\\"Q_from_theta\\\"](qt, theta)\\nS, At, Delta, Nd, P = ns[\\\"action\\\"](qtheta)\\nS.backward()\\ngrad = float(Nd.detach()),\\n    \\\"P_chiral\\\": float(P.detach()),\\n    \\\"gradient_norm\\\": grad,\\n}\\n\\nchecks = {\\n    \\\"frobenius\\\": float(fro),\\n    \\\"frobenius_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(fro) - locked[\\\"frobenius_error\\\"]),\\n    \\\"Jarlskog\\\": float(jarl),\\n    \\\"Jarlskog_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(jarl) - locked[\\\"Jarlskog\\\"]),\\n    \\\"s12_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(s12) - locked[\\\"s12\\\"]),\\n    \\\"s23_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(s23) - locked[\\\"s23\\\"]),\\n    \\\"s13_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(s13) - locked[\\\"s13\\\"]),\\n    \\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\": float(np.max(np.abs(vabs - np.asarray(locked[\\\"V_abs\\\"])))),\\n    \\\"row_perm\\\": list(row_perm),\\n    \\\"col_perm\\\": list(col_perm),\\n    \\\"frame_action\\\": action_values,\\n    \\\"frame_action_max_diff\\\": max(\\n        abs(action_values[k] - data[\\\"frame_action\\\"][k])\\n        for k in (\\\"S\\\", \\\"A_twist\\\", \\\"Delta\\\", \\\"N_d\\\", \\\"P_chiral\\\", \\\"gradient_norm\\\")\\n    ),\\n}\\nchecks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] = (\\n    checks[\\\"frobenius_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"Jarlskog_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s12_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s23_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s13_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-8\\n    and checks[\\\"frame_action_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-10\\n)\\nprint(json.dumps(checks, indent=2, sort_keys=True))\\nprint(\\\"VERIFICATION_RESULT:\\\", \\\"PASS\\\" if checks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] else ## Short answer: **No—not for the exact \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) result.**

We recovered substantial **mathematical construction**, but not the historical fit’s reproducible derivation.

### **STRUCTURE RECOVERED**

- `build_J_reproducible.py` derives the associator-to-Jordan lift:
  \[
  x=[u_1,u_2,H],\quad y=[u_2,u_3,H],\quad z=[u_3,u_1,H],
  \]
  with diagonals built from \(\|x\|^2,\|y\|^2,\|z\|^2\).
- It now constructs valid \(J_u,J_d,J_\nu,J_e\) from any supplied frame; today’s run returned `STRUCT_OK` for all four.
- `fts_moment_map_extraction.py` reconstructs the documented FTS state, symplectic form, compact moment map, and fitted \(r,s\) wedge.

### **EXACT PROVENANCE NOT RECOVERED**

Still missing:

1. the original full \(7\times7\) frame \(Q_0\);
2. the exact \(J_u,J_d\) generated from that frame;
3. the deleted original `hermes-verify-general-fts-state.py`;
4. its crucial `state_H_fast`/observable extraction code;
5. an invariant principle deriving \(Q_0\) or \(r,s\) rather than fitting them.

The existing `locked_jordan_data.json` explicitly labels itself:

> `constructed_representative_CKM_BY_CONSTRUCTION_NOT_DERIVATION`

and says:

> `upstream_frame_Q: NEVER CHECKPOINTED`

### **LIVE NUMERICAL CHECK: DOES NOT RECOVER THE FIT**

I reran both surviving replacement paths:

- `build_J_reproducible.py`, using its documented substitute frame:
  \[
  \text{error}=0.2198983,\qquad J_{\rm CP}=0.
  \]

- `fts_moment_map_extraction.py`, using the reconstructed locked matrices:
  \[
  \text{reported error}=0.6353836,\qquad
  J_{\rm CP}/J_{\rm target}=-229.10.
  \]

Neither approaches \(6.13\times10^{-6}\).

The FTS script prints `ALL_PASS: True`, but that only means its algebraic self-tests passed and the CKM routine returned status `RUN`; it does **not** assert that the CKM result passed.

## Correct updated statement

> We recovered the associator-to-Jordan construction and a replacement implementation of the documented FTS moment-map equations. However, these do not reproduce the historical \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) diagnostic fit. The original \(Q_0\), its exact \(J_u,J_d\), and the original observable-generating code remain unavailable. Therefore the number is still an **unverified historical diagnostic fit**, not a reproducible derivation.

Even if recovered, it would remain a **fit**, because the complex \(r,s\) values and physical frame were numerically selected using empirical CKM information. The honest closure is either rerun the full \(SO(7)\) optimization with checkpointing, or remove/downgrade the \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) claim.

[{"type": "message", "role": "assistant", "status": "completed", "content": [{"type": "output_text", "text": "## Short answer: **No—not for the exact \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) result.**

We recovered substantial **mathematical construction**, but not the historical fit’s reproducible derivation.

### **STRUCTURE RECOVERED**

- `build_J_reproducible.py` derives the associator-to-Jordan lift:
  \[
  x=[u_1,u_2,H],\quad y=[u_2,u_3,H],\quad z=[u_3,u_1,H],
  \]
  with diagonals built from \(\|x\|^2,\|y\|^2,\|z\|^2\).
- It now constructs valid \(J_u,J_d,J_\nu,J_e\) from any supplied frame; today’s run returned `STRUCT_OK` for all four.
- `fts_moment_map_extraction.py` reconstructs the documented FTS state, symplectic form, compact moment map, and fitted \(r,s\) wedge.

### **EXACT PROVENANCE NOT RECOVERED**

Still missing:

1. the original full \(7\times7\) frame \(Q_0\);
2. the exact \(J_u,J_d\) generated from that frame;
3. the deleted original `hermes-verify-general-fts-state.py`;
4. its crucial `state_H_fast`/observable extraction code;
5. an invariant principle deriving \(Q_0\) or \(r,s\) rather than fitting them.

The existing `locked_jordan_data.json` explicitly labels itself:

> `constructed_representative_CKM_BY_CONSTRUCTION_NOT_DERIVATION`

and says:

> `upstream_frame_Q: NEVER CHECKPOINTED`

### **LIVE NUMERICAL CHECK: DOES NOT RECOVER THE FIT**

I reran both surviving replacement paths:

- `build_J_reproducible.py`, using its documented substitute frame:
  \[
  \text{error}=0.2198983,\qquad J_{\rm CP}=0.
  \]

- `fts_moment_map_extraction.py`, using the reconstructed locked matrices:
  \[
  \text{reported error}=0.6353836,\qquad
  J_{\rm CP}/J_{\rm target}=-229.10.
  \]

Neither approaches \(6.13\times10^{-6}\).

The FTS script prints `ALL_PASS: True`, but that only means its algebraic self-tests passed and the CKM routine returned status `RUN`; it does **not** assert that the CKM result passed.

## Correct updated statement

> We recovered the associator-to-Jordan construction and a replacement implementation of the documented FTS moment-map equations. However, these do not reproduce the historical \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) diagnostic fit. The original \(Q_0\), its exact \(J_u,J_d\), and the original observable-generating code remain unavailable. Therefore the number is still an **unverified historical diagnostic fit**, not a reproducible derivation.

Even if recovered, it would remain a **fit**, because the complex \(r,s\) values and physical frame were numerically selected using empirical CKM information. The honest closure is either rerun the full \(SO(7)\) optimization with checkpointing, or remove/downgrade the \(6.13\times10^{-6}\) claim."}], "id": "msg_071f367ba0e10612016a551ea233a08191ad20c161fbfc195b", "phase": "final_answer"}]

Use when validating mathematical, physical, or algebraic claims against local artifacts.", "tags": [], "related_skills": [], "content": "---
name: gate-verification
description: Systematic claim verification using explicit gate structures (claim, construction, sub-gates, honesty boundary, pass/fail). Use when validating mathematical, physical, or algebraic claims against local artifacts.
---

# Gate Verification

Systematic evidence-backed verification of claims using explicit gate structures.

## Trigger

Use when:
- User asks to "verify", "check", "audit", or "test" a claim
- A mathematical/physical/algebraic claim needs explicit PASS/FAIL evidence
- A literature survey must distinguish formalism, EFT examples, named geometries, uplifted solutions, and Hessian-ready constructions
- Required artifacts must be confirmed before running verification
- User establishes a "gate" pattern with sub-criteria

Do NOT use for:
- Simple syntax checks or linting
- Vague "does this work" questions without explicit criteria

## Required Workflow

### 1. Confirm Workspace Paths First

**Before any verification:**
- Identify the user's canonical local repo path
- Verify it exists and is accessible
- Do NOT assume `/mnt/data`, `/tmp`, `/workspace`, or any sandbox path is mounted locally
- If user says "use local repo as source of truth", confirm paths against that repo only

Pitfall: Silently substituting assumed sandbox paths for local repos breaks verification and wastes compute.

### 2. Verify Required Artifacts Before Running

List all required files explicitly. Check existence one-by-one if needed.

If any file is missing:
- STOP
- List the missing files
- Ask user to copy/download them into the canonical repo
- Do NOT proceed with partial substitution unless user explicitly approves

### 3. Define Gate Structure

Every verification must have:

```markdown
## Gate [X] — [Name]

**Claim under test:** [exact statement]

**Construction:** [how the claim is instantiated from verified components]

| Sub-gate | PASS criterion |
|---|---|
| [A1] — [name] | [exact pass test] |
| [A2] — [name] | [exact pass test] |
| [A3] — [name] | [exact pass test] |

**Honesty boundary:** [what passing does NOT prove]
```

### 4. Run Verification and Record Evidence

- Use the canonical local repo as working directory
- Run the verification script/module
- Capture stdout and exit code
- Read generated artifacts (JSON, MD reports)
- Do NOT claim verification without reading the actual output

### 5. Report Explicit PASS/FAIL

Report format:
- Gate number and name
- Sub-gate results (PASS/FAIL for each)
- Overall verdict: PASS or FAIL
- Evidence: key numbers from artifacts
- Honesty boundary: what the result does NOT prove

Keep user-facing verification summaries concise. Lead with the result and the smallest useful evidence set; avoid long explanatory digressions unless the user asks for the teaching version.

## Ad-Hoc Verification Scripts

When creating temporary verification scripts:

- Use OS temp directory: `C:\Users\<user>\AppData\Local\Temp` on Windows
- Filename prefix: `hermes-verify-`
- Script purpose: targeted check of the changed behavior
- If a post-edit system check says no canonical command was detected, run the focused temp script even if you already ran tests; label the result explicitly as **ad-hoc verification**, not suite/CI green.
- After running: clean up the temp script
- If system feedback flags specific changed paths, verify those exact paths even if they are accidental copies; do not silently switch the verification target to the intended canonical path. If the flagged copy depends on canonical repo modules, import those dependencies explicitly and state that boundary in the verification summary.
- See `references/changed-path-ad-hoc-verification.md` for the changed-path verification pattern and tiny7m example.

Template pattern:
```python
import sys, json
from pathlib import Path

repo = Path(r"D:\Projects\...")
artifact = repo / "results.json"

ok = True
def check(cond, label):
    global ok
    if not cond:
        ok = False
        print(f"FAIL: {label}")
    else:
        print(f"OK: {label}")

# assertions...

print("VERIFICATION_RESULT:", "PASS" if ok else "FAIL")
sys.exit(0 if ok else 1)
```

## Pitfalls

1. **Sandbox path assumption**: Never assume `/mnt/data`, `/tmp`, `/workspace`, or cloud sandbox paths are locally mounted. Confirm with user first.

2. **Silent file substitution**: If a required artifact is missing, report it and stop. Do not substitute a "similar" file without explicit user approval.

3. **Synthetic dry-run ≠ real-data run**: When the user asks to run/download/evaluate **real** data, do not answer by generating synthetic-shaped data or a dry-run unless the user explicitly approves that fallback. First check for the real file/source and credentials; if absent, state the blocker plainly and ask for the missing credential/path. Synthetic harness validation may be useful, but label it as a separate preparatory check, not the requested real-data result.

4. **Claim without evidence**: Never say "verified" or "PASS" without reading the actual output artifacts. A script that runs is not a script that passes.

4a. **Harness says PASS while the claim fails**: Audit the pass-condition logic itself. A status such as `RUN`, `COMPLETED`, or `DATA_LOADED` proves only that execution occurred; it must not satisfy a scientific gate unless the claimed numerical thresholds are asserted. Recompute and print the headline metrics independently, inspect artifact metadata for circularity/proxy/reconstruction warnings, and fail the gate when those warnings invalidate provenance. Also verify that metric names match formulas (`sqrt(mean(error^2))` is RMSE, not a Frobenius norm).

5. **Over-broad verification**: Run targeted checks, not full suites, unless user requests it.

6. **Missing cleanup**: Remove temp verification scripts after use.

7. **Reproducibility gate on cited numeric anchors**: A claim that leans on a previously-reported number (e.g. "fro~1e-5 CKM", "99% accuracy") must be gated by re-deriving that number from the **actual artifact** before you build on it. A docstring/comment/summary line is not the anchor — scan the real trained checkpoints / run the real path. If the number is unreproducible (here: best frame-overlap fro = 0.32 across all 32 `.pt` files, vs claimed 1e-5), the gate FAILS and you must NOT align, refactor, or "complete the path" to a phantom anchor. Report the negative gate and the best-achieved floor instead. Reusable recipe: `verified-algebra-workflow` → `references/ckm-anchor-reproducibility-gate.md`.

8. **User "GO FOR IT" / explicit direction does not waive the evidence gate**: When the user says "align Gate C to stage_h" or "go for it", treat it as authorization to *proceed with the verified plan*, not as permission to skip verification of the premise. If verifying the premise reveals the cited anchor is false/unreproducible, stop and report the negative gate rather than executing the alignment. Executing a refactor onto a phantom anchor is fabrication-adjacent, not obedience.

9. **Placeholder values are effectively missing artifacts**: A required target input file may exist but still contain unverified placeholder/memory/default values, or the user states no exact-cited target exists yet. Treat it as missing for the currently gated claim: stop asking for it, report the blocker plainly, and preserve any current analytical residue or derivation already obtained instead of rereading the blocker path.
10. **Blocked exact-cited gate fallback**: When a physics/algebraic gate is blocked by a missing exact target, prefer reporting concise analytical residue over continued searches for the absent input. If a derivation or invariance result exists, report it with explicit status labels—do not bury it under additional searches or suggest unverified completions.

11. **GUT/Higgs analogy promoted to identity**: A fitted coefficient matrix that resembles a two-Higgs or GUT boundary is not thereby a Clebsch matrix. Separate fixed representation tensors, continuous VEV ratios, unitary Higgs-basis rotations, nonunitary heavy-field matching, and family Yukawa matrices. Run the necessary `C†C ∝ I` test for a literal two-state mixer, then require a named representation chain and primitive-span residual test. Backward RG is a matching operation unless an independently fixed UV boundary and contraction analysis establish selection. See `references/e6-higgs-boundary-audit.md`.

12. **Speculative source promoted to solution**: A real preprint record, generic mechanism class, social-media summary, or formula that matches data does not by itself resolve a model-specific open problem. Verify source status and accessible evidence level, preserve the canonical meaning of every symbol, distinguish encoding/fit/relation/selector, and recompute headline numerical accuracy. If full text is unavailable but DOI metadata or an abstract is accessible, quote only that layer and state that the derivation was not audited. See `references/speculative-theory-source-audit.md`.

## References

- `references/gate-template.md` — standard gate template
- `references/theory-literature-survey-gates.md` — evidence ladder, metadata triangulation, Hessian discipline, causal-map prerequisite, exact discrete-data checks, and next-gate candidate selection for mathematical-physics literature surveys
- `references/speculative-theory-source-audit.md` — source-status triangulation, symbol-integrity checks, mechanism-versus-implementation discipline, fit/selector classification, and numerical claim rechecks for speculative external theory documents
- `references/group-theory-matrix-identity-audits.md` — object classification, primary-equation extraction, structural matrix tests, and verdict rules for claims equating fitted arrays with CG tensors, Higgs mixing, VEV ratios, vectorlike mixing, or Yukawa matrices
- `references/e6-higgs-boundary-audit.md` — E6/Albert real-form correction, literal two-Higgs unitarity test, primitive-span matching, RG-selector versus matcher gate, and physical 2HDM completion requirements
- `references/compact-g2-standard-model-audit.md` — hard conjunction for compactification-to-phenomenology claims, frontier separation, flux-scan prerequisite, and exact rational tadpole/intersection audit pattern
- `references/real-data-vs-synthetic-pitfall.md` — avoid substituting synthetic fixtures when the user requested real/sandbox data; includes Pinch sandbox auth/event endpoint notes
- `references/pmns-target-placeholders.md` — placeholder-valued target inputs are hard stops equal to missing artifacts
- `scripts/verify_gate_results.py` — reusable ad-hoc verification script

{"content": "1|# Locked historical FTS diagnostic fit
2|
3|## Verdict
4|
5|The July 11 Hermes result is locked as a **reproduced historical diagnostic fit**, not a CKM derivation.
6|
7|Machine-readable source of truth: `locked_historical_fts_diagnostic_fit.json`.
8|
9|Recovered executable: `historical_hermes_general_fts_state_recovered.py`.
10|
11|Structural verifier: `python verify_locked_historical_fts_fit.py`.
12|
13|## What is locked
14|
15|- the recovered `Q_star` and its column interpretation;
16|- the historical associator-to-Jordan construction of `J_u,J_d`;
17|- the full compact octonion coefficients of `J_u,J_d`;
18|- the stationary frame-action statistics;
19|- fitted complex coefficients `r,s`;
20|- the recorded CKM magnitudes, Jarlskog invariant, angles, and error;
21|- database session/message provenance and source hashes.
22|
23|## Claim firewall
24|
25|Safe statement:
26|
27|> A recovered and rerun Hermes pipeline constructs `J_u,J_d` from a seeded, action-minimized shared frame. A subsequent four-real-parameter, CKM-targeted complex Jordan-slot fit reproduces the CKM reference with Frobenius error `6.1281124199e-6` and the target Jarlskog invariant.
28|
29|Unsafe statement:
30|
31|> The octonionic/FTS construction derives or predicts CKM.
32|
33|The unsafe statement is not supported because CKM information enters both the `Q0` warm start and the `r,s` objective.
34|
35|## Derivation gate
36|
37|The diagnostic fit is now frozen and must not be changed during a derivation attempt. A candidate selector passes only if:
38|
39|1. its objective contains no CKM magnitudes, mixing angles, Jarlskog target, fitted `r,s`, or distance to `Q_star`;
40|2. all coefficients and boundary charges are specified before looking at CKM scoring;
41|3. it selects `Q,r,s` from multiple independent starts;
42|4. CKM is evaluated only after selection;
43|5. the result is stable to convention checks and small numerical perturbations;
44|6. parameter counting and null baselines show that success is not generic fitting capacity.
45|
46|## Already failed selector families
47|
48|The Hermes history records negative gates for:
49|
50|- direct normalized FTS/Jordan invariant actions in `r,s`;
51|- simple cross-norm, determinant, symplectic, and moment-map pseudoscalar combinations;
52|- self-attractor, coupled-plane, and primitive-charge closures tested in the initial full-FTS attractor attempt.
53|
54|These failures do not make derivation logically impossible. They rule out the obvious low-complexity completions. A credible next attempt needs independently justified two-Higgs/GUT boundary charges or a larger `E7` orbit/attractor construction—not another objective reverse-engineered from the fitted point.

{"content": "1|"""Verify the immutable, non-predictive parts of the recovered Hermes fit lock.
2|
3|This script deliberately does not optimize against CKM. It verifies that the
4|locked orthogonal frame reconstructs the recorded octonionic J_u and J_d.
5|"""
6|
7|from __future__ import annotations
8|
9|import json
10|from pathlib import Path
11|
12|import numpy as np
13|
14|
15|ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent
16|LOCK = ROOT / "locked_historical_fts_diagnostic_fit.json"
17|
18|
19|def qmul(x: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
20|    a, b, c, d = x
21|    e, f, g, h = y
22|    return np.array(
23|        [
24|            a * e - b * f - c * g - d * h,
25|            a * f + b * e + c * h - d * g,
26|            a * g - b * h + c * e + d * f,
27|            a * h + b * g - c * f + d * e,
28|        ]
29|    )
30|
31|
32|def qconj(x: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
33|    out = x.copy()
34|    out[1:] *= -1
35|    return out
36|
37|
38|def omul(x: np.ndarray, y: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
39|    a, b = x[:4], x[4:]
40|    c, d = y[:4], y[4:]
41|    return np.r_[qmul(a, c) - qmul(qconj(d), b), qmul(d, a) + qmul(b, qconj(c))]
42|
43|
44|def assoc(a: np.ndarray, b: np.ndarray, h: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
45|    def pure(v: np.ndarray) -> np.ndarray:
46|        return np.r_[0.0, v]
47|
48|    return (omul(omul(pure(a), pure(b)), pure(h)) - omul(pure(a), omul(pure(b), pure(h))))[1:]
49|
50|
51|def build_compact_j(gen: np.ndarray, h: np.ndarray) -> dict[str, np.ndarray]:
52|    a01 = assoc(gen[:, 0], gen[:, 1], h)
53|    a12 = assoc(gen[:, 1], gen[:, 2], h)
54|    a20 = assoc(gen[:, 2], gen[:, 0], h)
55|    diagonal = np.array(
56|        [
57|            np.dot(a20, a20) + np.dot(a01, a01),
58|            np.dot(a01, a01) + np.dot(a12, a12),
59|            np.dot(a20, a20) + np.dot(a12, a12),
60|        ]
61|    )
62|    return {"diagonal": diagonal, "a01": a01, "a12": a12, "a20": a20}
63|
64|
65|def main() -> int:
66|    data = json.loads(LOCK.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
67|    q = np.asarray(data["Q_star"], dtype=float)
68|    gen = q[:, :3]
69|
70|    checks: dict[str, float | bool | str] = {
71|        "status_is_diagnostic_not_derivation": data["status"].endswith("NOT_DERIVATION"),
72|        "q_orthogonality_max_abs": float(np.max(np.abs(q.T @ q - np.eye(7)))),
73|        "q_determinant": float(np.linalg.det(q)),
74|    }
75|
76|    for label, col in (("J_u", 3), ("J_d", 5)):
77|        got = build_compact_j(gen, q[:, col])
78|        expected = data[label]
79|        checks[f"{label}_diagonal_max_abs"] = float(
80|            np.max(np.abs(got["diagonal"] - np.asarray(expected["diagonal"])))
81|        )
82|        for name in ("a01", "a12", "a20"):
83|            checks[f"{label}_{name}_max_abs"] = float(
84|                np.max(np.abs(got[name] - np.asarray(expected[f"{name}_e1_to_e7"])))
85|            )
86|
87|    tolerances = [v for k, v in checks.items() if k.endswith("max_abs")]
88|    passed = (
89|        bool(checks["status_is_diagnostic_not_derivation"])
90|        and abs(checks["q_determinant"] - 1.0) < 1e-10
91|        and all(float(v) < 1e-10 for v in tolerances)
92|    )
93|    checks["all_pass"] = passed
94|    print(json.dumps(checks, indent=2, sort_keys=True))
95|    return 0 if passed else 1
96|
97|
98|if __name__ == "__main__":
99|    raise SystemExit(main())

61|def Atwist(Q):

139|def reconstruct_H_from_mu(mu_func):

\Omega,\ \tau,\ T\in u(3)_{\rm gen},\ H_u,\ H_d,\ \text{and diagonalization are now coded and tested.}
}
\]

Verdict:

\[
\boxed{
\text{FTS compact moment-map projection works as a projection, but the simplest sector states do not complete CKM.}
}
\]

---

# 1. What I implemented

Freudenthal state:

\[
\Psi_s=(1,1,X_s,\overline{X_s}).
\]

Real structure:

\[
\tau(\alpha,\beta,X,Y)
=
(\overline\beta,\overline\alpha,\overline Y,\overline X).
\]

Symplectic form:

\[
\Omega((a,b,X,Y),(c,d,Z,W))
=
ad-bc+\langle X,W\rangle-\langle Y,Z\rangle.
\]

For a sector state,

\[
\Psi=(1,1,X,\overline X),
\]

this becomes:

\[
\Omega(\tau\Psi,T\Psi)
=
\langle X,\overline{dX}\rangle
-
\langle\overline X,dX\rangle.
\]

Moment map:

\[
\boxed{
\mu_\Psi(T)
=
\frac{i}{2}
\Omega(\tau\Psi,T\Psi).
}
\]

I represented

\[
T\in u(3)_{\rm gen}
\]

as

\[
T=iA,
\qquad
A=A^\dagger.
\]

Then reconstructed the Hermitian generation observable \(H_\Psi\) from

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Tr}_3(H_\Psi A)=\mu_\Psi(iA).
}
\]

The \(u(3)_{\rm gen}\) action was applied to the generation frame columns \(g_a\), not as a post-hoc matrix patch:

\[
g\mapsto g\exp(\epsilon T).
\]

---

# 2. Verification diagnostics

The moment-map reconstruction passed.

Random Hermitian probe errors:

\[
\sim 4\times10^{-14}\text{ to }2\times10^{-13}.
\]

So the extracted \(H_\Psi\) really matches

\[
\mu_\Psi(T).
\]

Action-minimized frame reproduced:

\[
S_*=-0.473691374068,
\]

\[
\Delta-2N_d=-0.352199185598,
\]

\[
P_{\rm chiral}=0.242984376941,
\]

\[
\|\nabla S_*\|\approx1.78\times10^{-7}.
\]

# 5. Complexified generation variants

I tested complexified generation states:

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(icK),
\]

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(icL),
\]

and split variants:

\[
g_u\mapsto g\exp(-icG/2),
\qquad
g_d\mapsto g\exp(+icG/2).
\]

They do generate complex Hermitian \(H_u,H_d\), hence nonzero CP.

But they do not land on CKM.

Representative example:

\[
g_d\mapsto g\exp(i0.5K):
\]

\[
|V|\approx

\end{pmatrix}.
\]

\|\,|V|-|V_{\rm CKM}|\,\|_F
\approx0.22784,
\]

\[
\frac{|J|}{J_{\rm target}}
\approx0.69086.
\]

Best tested complex magnitude case was split \(G=L,\ c=1\):

|V|\approx

but CP is far too large:

\approx11.49.

# 6. Gate verdict

Projection implementation:

\boxed{\text{PASS}}

Physics completion:

\[
\boxed{\text{FAIL for the simplest tested } \Psi_s=(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s}) \text{ states.}}
\]

The important thing is that this is now no longer vague. The actual projection is coded and verified:

\[
\boxed{
\operatorname{Tr}_3(H_\Psi A)
=
\frac{i}{2}
\Omega(\tau\Psi,iA\Psi).
}
\]

And it gives real, diagonalizable Hermitian \(H_u,H_d\).

But the sector state itself is still incomplete.

---

# 7. What this means physically

The observable projection is now correct, but the **state inserted into the projection** is not yet the full physical FTS state.

The simple choice

\[
\Psi_s=(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s})
\]

is too poor.

It either gives:

- real states: decent structure, no CP;
- compact real rotations: better magnitudes, still no CP;
- complexified one-generator states: CP appears, but overshoots or distorts hierarchy.

So the next missing object is not the projection anymore.

It is:

\[
\boxed{
\text{the physical Freudenthal state } \Psi_u,\Psi_d.
}
\]

Likely it must include:

\[
(\alpha,\beta)
\]

nontrivially, and/or both Jordan slots:

\[
\Psi_s=(\alpha_s,\beta_s,X_s,Y_s),
\]

not merely

\[
(1,1,J_s,\overline{J_s}).
\]

in range(a0+1,3):\n            mre=mu_func(HERM[idx]); mim=mu_func(HERM[idx+1]); idx+=2\n            H[a0,b0]=0.5*mre+0.5j*mim; H[b0,a0]=np.conjugate(H[a0,b0])\n    H=0.5*(H+H.conj().T)\n    # random probe diag\n    rng=np.random.default_rng(44); err=0.0\n    for _ in range(5):\n        Z=rng.normal(size=(3,3))+1j*rng.normal(size=(3,3)); Aherm=0.5*(Z+Z.conj().T)\n        err=max(err,abs(mu_func(Aherm)-np.trace(H@Aherm).real))\n    return H,err\nclass SlotCache:\n    def __init__(self,gen,vacs):\n        self.gen=gen; self.vacs=vacs; self.J=[build_J_np(gen,H) for H in vacs]\n        self.dT=[]; self.dTbar=[]\n        for Aherm in HERM:\n            T=1j*Aherm\n            self.dT.append([dJ_by_T_np(gen,H,T) for H in vacs])\n            self.dTbar.append([dJ_by_T_np(gen,H,np.conjugate(T)) for H in vacs])\n    def state_H(self,Xcoef,Ycoef):\n        # Xcoef,Ycoef length n complex; T acts on X, conjugate representation on Y to commute with tau\n        X=sum(Xcoef[i]*self.J[i] for i in range(len(self.J)))\n        Y=sum(Ycoef[i]*self.J[i] for i in range(len(self.J)))\n        def mu_for_A(Aherm):\n            # compute d for arbitrary probe not in HERM for diagnostics\n            T=1j*Aherm; Tbar=np.conjugate(T)\n            dX=sum(Xcoef[i]*dJ_by_T_np(self.gen,self.vacs[i],T) for i in range(len(self.J)))\n            dY=sum(Ycoef[i]*dJ_by_T_np(self.gen,self.vacs[i],Tbar) for i in range(len(self.J)))\n            omega=innerJ_np(np.conjugate(Y),dY)-innerJ_np(np.conjugate(X),dX)\n            return np.real_if_close((1j/2)*omega,tol=1000).real\n        H,err=reconstruct_H_from_mu(mu_for_A)\n        return H,err\n# faster no random diagnostics for optimization\ndef omega=innerJ_np(np.conjugate(Y),dY)-innerJ_np(np.conjugate(X),dX)\n        vals.append(np.real_if_close((1j/2)*omega,tol=1000).real)\n    H=np.zeros((3,3),dtype=np.complex128)\n    for a0 in range(3): H[a0,a0]=vals[a0]\n    idx=3\n    for a0 in range(3):\n        for b0 in range(a0+1,3):\n            H[a0,b0]=0.5*vals[idx]+0.5j*vals[idx+1]; H[b0,a0]=np.conjugate(H[a0,b0]); idx+=2\n    return fro,J,s12,s23,s13,W\nprint('TARGET delta',delta_t,'J_target',J_target,'scipy',HAVE_SCIPY,flush=True)\n# reproduce same Qstar\nraw=torch.randn(7,7,generator=torch.Generator().manual_seed(12005),requires_grad=True)\nopt=torch.optim.Adam([raw],lr=.03); "type": "function", "function": {"name": "skill_manage", "arguments": "{\"action\":\"write_file\",\"file_content\":\"# General FTS state gate: scalar slots vs complex Jordan slots\\n\\nUse after map.\\n\\n## Claim under test\\n\\nThe user proposed replacing the minimal sector state\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_s = (1,1,J_s,conj(J_s))\\n```\\n\\nby a more physical FTS state with nontrivial scalar slots and independent Jordan slots, e.g.\\n\\n```text\\nPsi = (v_u,v_d,J_u,J_d).\\n```\\n\\n## Construction\\n\\nKept the already verified FTS compact moment map:\\n\\n```text\\nmu_Psi(T) = (i/2) Omega(tau(Psi),T Psi),  T in u(3)_gen\\n```\\n\\nwith\\n\\n```text\\nOmega((a,b,X,Y),(c,d,Z,W)) = a d - b c + <X,W> - <Y,Z>\\ntau(alpha,beta,X,Y) = (conj(beta),conj(alpha),conj(Y),conj(X))\\n```\\n\\nFor compact generation probes `T=iA`, `A=A†`, the implementation used the representation commuting with `tau`:\\n\\n```text\\nT X = dX,\\nT Y = conj-representation derivative dY.\\n```\\n\\nThe Hermitian generation observable was reconstructed from\\n\\n```text\\nTr_3(H_Psi A) = mu_Psi(iA).\\n```\\n\\n## Action-minimized frame\\n\\nSame stationary point:\\n\\n```text\\nS      = -0.47369137406837525\\nAtwist = -0.3521991855981212\\nDelta  = -0.10808374021302673\\nN_d    =  0.12205772269254724\\nP      =  0.24298437694050817\\ngrad   =  1.782443763352243e-07\\n```\\n\\n## Key algebraic finding: scalar slots are inert for u(3)_gen\\n\\nFor `T in u(3)_gen` acting only on generation/Jordan slots,\\n\\n```text\\nT alpha = 0,\\nT beta  = 0.\\n```\\n\\nTherefore the scalar part of the symplectic form contributes\\n\\n```text\\nalpha * delta(beta) - beta * delta(alpha) = 0.\\n```\\n\\nNumerical check:\\n\\n```text\\nALPHA_BETA_INVARIANCE max_delta_H_over_random_scalars 0.0\\n```\\n\\nSo changing `alpha,beta` alone cannot change CKM under this moment-map projection. They may affect a larger E7 state-selection action or central/trace sector, but they do not alter the generation eigenvectors for `u(3)_gen`.\\n\\n## Independent raw slots Psi=(alpha,beta,J_u,J_d)\\n\\nA single combined state\\n\\n```text\\nPsi=(alpha,beta,J_u,J_d)\\n```\\n\\nproduces only one moment-map observable `H`, not two sector observables. The computed combined observable was real:\\n\\n```text\\nH_combined ≈\\n[[173.979543,   2.074161, -20.367384],\\n [  2.074161, 176.035057,  -0.944810],\\n [-20.367384,  -0.944810, 172.392181]]\\n```\\n\\nSlot-separated observables from `X=J_u` and `Y=J_d` reproduce the earlier real-sector no-CP result:\\n\\n```text\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.99562284,0.09034180,0.02394843],\\n [0.08918675,0.99497214,0.04556501],\\n [0.02794445,0.04322968,0.99867427]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 0.195598\\nJ/J_target = 0\\n```\\n\\nSwapping combined states `Psi_u=(J_u,J_d)`, `Psi_d=(J_d,J_u)` gives identity mixing and `J=0`.\\n\\n## Non-real independent slot search without slot interference\\n\\nDiagnostic ansatz:\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_u=(J_u, r J_d),\\nPsi_d=(s J_u, J_d)\\n```\\n\\ncan fit real magnitudes well but still gives no CP because each slot contribution scales without complex interference:\\n\\n```text\\nbest r ≈ -0.247973 - 0.378564 i\\nbest s ≈ -0.988994 + 0.492332 i\\n\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.97437895,0.22472872,0.00909220],\\n [0.22491247,0.97354413,0.04032631],\\n [0.00021082,0.04133806,0.99914520]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 0.010071\\nJ/J_target ≈ 5.8e-18\\n```\\n\\n## Decisive positive diagnostic: complex Jordan-slot mixing\\n\\nAllow the sector states to be tau-real but with complex linear mixing inside the Jordan slot:\\n\\n```text\\nPsi_u:\\n  X_u = J_u + r J_d\\n  Y_u = conj(X_u)\\n\\nPsi_d:\\n  X_d = s J_u + J_d\\n  Y_d = conj(X_d)\\n```\\n\\nA 4-real-parameter diagnostic search found:\\n\\n```text\\nr ≈ 0.3887831255168539 + 0.28228702817500606 i\\ns ≈ 0.23729498924317205 - 0.5117538711322562 i\\n```\\n\\nand produced essentially exact CKM magnitudes and J:\\n\\n```text\\n|V| ≈\\n[[0.97435246,0.22499704,0.00369001],\\n [0.22486405,0.97349226,0.04182082],\\n [0.00856999,0.04109930,0.99911831]]\\n\\nFrobenius ≈ 6.13e-06\\nJ ≈ 3.076559155801642e-05\\nJ/J_target ≈ 1.0000000017619248\\n\\ns12 ≈ 0.2249985724\\ns23 ≈ 0.0418211054\\ns13 ≈ 0.0036900121\\n```\\n\\n## Verdict\\n\\nProjection gate:\\n\\n```text\\nPASS: FTS moment map still works.\\nPASS: alpha,beta invariance under u(3)_gen verified.\\nPASS: independent Jordan slots implemented.\\nPASS: complex tau-real Jordan-slot mixing can reproduce CKM as a diagnostic fit.\\n```\\n\\nPhysics/derivation gate:\\n\\n```text\\nFAIL: scalar slots alone do not solve CKM.\\nFAIL: raw Psi=(v_u,v_d,J_u,J_d) does not itself provide two CKM sector observables or CP.\\nNOT YET PROVEN: the complex mixing coefficients r,s are derived by the action.\\n```\\n\\n## Honesty boundary\\n\\nThe near-exact CKM result is a fitted 4-real-parameter diagnostic, not a zero-parameter derivation. Its value is that it localizes the missing physics precisely: the physical FTS state must contain complex tau-real Jordan-slot mixing of `J_u` and `J_d`. The next gate is to derive `r,s` from an invariant FTS state-selection equation, not to tune them to CKM.\\n\\nPossible next action: extend the action from `S(Q)` on the shared frame to `S(Q,r,s)` on the FTS state and test whether its stationary equations recover the fitted `r,s` without CKM input.\\n\",\"file_path\":\"references/general-fts-state-jordan-slot-mixing-gate.md\",\"name\":\"verified-algebra-workflow\"}"}}]}], "messages_before": 5, "messages_after": 5}

= rec[:8]\\nlocked = data[\\\"diagnostic_fit\\\"]\\n\\n# Recompute the frame action and its local 21-angle gradient at the locked Q.\\nqt = torch.tensor(q, dtype=torch.float64)\\ntheta = \\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\": float(np.max(np.abs(vabs - np.asarray(locked[\\\"V_abs\\\"])))),\\n    \\\"row_perm\\\": list(row_perm),\\n    \\\"col_perm\\\": list(col_perm),\\n    \\\"frame_action\\\": \\\"P_chiral\\\", \\\"gradient_norm\\\")\\n    ),\\n}\\nchecks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] = (\\n    checks[\\\"frobenius_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"Jarlskog_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s12_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s23_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"s13_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-8\\n    and checks[\\\"frame_action_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-10\\n)\\nprint(json.dumps(checks, indent=2, sort_keys=True))\\nprint(\\\"VERIFICATION_RESULT:\\\", \\\"PASS\\\" if checks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] else grad_abs_diff = abs(grad - lock[\\\"frame_action\\\"][\\\"gradient_norm\\\"])\\nchecks = {\\n    \\\"frobenius\\\": float(fro),\\n    \\\"frobenius_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(fro) - fit[\\\"frobenius_error\\\"]),\\n    \\\"Jarlskog\\\": float(J),\\n    \\\"Jarlskog_abs_diff\\\": abs(float(J) - fit[\\\"Jarlskog\\\"]),\\n    \\\"angles_max_diff\\\": max(abs(float(s12)-fit[\\\"s12\\\"]), abs(float(s23)-fit[\\\"s23\\\"]), abs(float(s13)-fit[\\\"s13\\\"])),\\n    \\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\": float(np.max(np.abs(W - np.asarray(fit[\\\"V_abs\\\"])))),\\n    \\\"row_perm\\\": list(pr), \\\"col_perm\\\": list(pc),\\n    \\\"frame_action_scalar_max_diff\\\": scalar_action_max_diff,\\n    \\\"frame_gradient_recomputed\\\": grad,\\n    \\\"frame_gradient_abs_diff\\\": grad_abs_diff,\\n}\\nchecks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] = (\\n    checks[\\\"frobenius_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12 and checks[\\\"Jarlskog_abs_diff\\\"] < 1e-12\\n    and checks[\\\"angles_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-12 and checks[\\\"V_abs_max_diff\\\"] < 1e-8\\n    and scalar_action_max_diff < 1e-12 and grad_abs_diff < 5e-9\\n)\\nprint(json.dumps(checks, indent=2, sort_keys=True))\\nprint(\\\"VERIFICATION_RESULT:\\\", \\\"PASS\\\" if checks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] else \\\"FAIL\\\")\\nraise SystemExit(0 if checks[\\\"all_pass\\\"] else 1)\\n\",\"path\":\"C:/Users/theai/AppData/Local/Temp/hermes-verify-locked-fts-direct.py\"}"}}]
